the fractional part of $ x $ is defined as the part of $ x $ after the decimal point, and it is always between 0 (inclusive) and 1 (exclusive). the inequality involves the sum of the fractional parts of $ x $, $ x^3 $, $ x^5 $, and so on, up to $ x^{99} $. the goal is to determine if there exists a real number $ x $ greater than 1 that is not an integer and satisfies this inequality.
